title: SACRAMENTO
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 32 aims to make current telehealth flexibilities permanent under California state law.",
    "Telehealth modalities have been critical in providing health care during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Access to healthcare should not depend upon a person's location, mobility, or income.",
    "Telehealth flexibilities have allowed patients to access the full spectrum of health care.",
    "Rural Californians have historically lacked equitable access to quality healthcare.",
    "Telehealth has maintained access to sexual and reproductive health care during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Telehealth has emerged as a critical pathway for patients to receive time-sensitive health care remotely.",
    "Telehealth adoption has been proven to improve equity and access to health services.",
    "Telehealth has reduced wait times to see providers.",
    "Telehealth allows patients to access services in ways that work for their individual circumstances.",
    "Telehealth has become an essential tool for physicians' practices during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Telehealth bridges access barriers by utilizing technology to connect patients to their care providers.",
    "Telehealth has the potential to be a great equalizer for healthcare access.",
    "Telehealth practices have shown to address health disparities, especially during the pandemic.",
    "Policies that support telehealth can help lower-income families overcome access barriers."
  ]
}
```
